food_subgroup: Fried chicken and potato combo platter

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined fried chicken wings and french fries platter. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest parent for the chicken wings component. The french fries component is not separately anchored.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW covers fried chicken wings as the dominant component. No composite code exists for a chicken wings and french fries combo platter in FoodEx2. French fries component is subsumed under the composite dish estimate.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific fried chicken wings and french fries combo. SNOMED CT 255620007 (Foods substance) is used as the broadest defensible parent anchor.

serving.description: 1 combo platter (approximately 4 chicken wings and a portion of french fries)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 350 g, comprising around 4 medium fried chicken wings (approximately 200 g) and a standard portion of french fries (approximately 150 g), as served at a Singapore kopitiam or casual dining outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central: Chicken wing, fried, coated (FDC ID 171477) and French fries, frozen, oven-heated (FDC ID 170720); proportioned at approximately 57% chicken wings and 43% french fries by weight
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this specific combo dish. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional blending of USDA FDC entries for deep-fried coated chicken wings (FDC ID 171477, approximately 57% of serving weight) and french fries oven-heated from frozen (FDC ID 170720, approximately 43% of serving weight). Values represent a plausible central estimate for this type of combo platter as served in Singapore casual dining. Sodium adjusted upward by approximately 10% to account for additional seasoning typical of Singapore-style preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of french fries and fried potato products; analogous estimate applied to composite dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific fried chicken wings and french fries combo. The GI of 65 is estimated as a weighted blend: french fries have a published GI of approximately 63–75 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) and fried chicken wings with batter coating have a GI of approximately 40–50 due to protein and fat dampening. A blended GI of 65 is applied, reflecting the dominant carbohydrate contribution from the french fries component (approximately 43% of serving weight). GL per serving calculated as (65 × 21.4 × 3.5) / 100 = 15.8 g, using 350 g serving and 21.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from seasoning, batter, and deep-frying process
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this dish as a single 350 g serving provides approximately 1470 mg sodium, representing over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (65) driven by the french fries component, combined with high total fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the french fries component contributes a medium GI of 65 and a glycemic load of approximately 15.8 g per standard serving, which may cause a moderate postprandial blood glucose rise; pairing with non-starchy vegetables may help blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its high total fat (15.2 g/100 g), saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g), trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and elevated sodium (420 mg/100 g), all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Fried Chicken Wings with French Fries (黄金鸡翅拼薯条, Huáng Jīn Jī Chì Pīn Shǔ Tiáo) is a popular combo platter served at Singapore kopitiams and casual Chinese-style eateries. The dish consists of deep-fried chicken wings coated in a seasoned batter that turns golden and crispy during frying, served alongside a portion of salted french fries. The chicken wings are typically marinated in soy sauce, garlic, and five-spice or similar Chinese seasonings before being coated in a light flour or cornstarch batter and deep-fried in hot oil until the skin is crunchy and the interior remains juicy. The french fries are either freshly cut or frozen-style, deep-fried or oven-heated, and seasoned with salt. The overall flavour profile is savoury, salty, and rich, with a satisfying contrast between the crispy battered chicken skin and the soft potato interior of the fries. Nutritionally, this dish is energy-dense at approximately 271 kcal per 100 g, with significant fat (15.2 g/100 g) and moderate carbohydrate (21.4 g/100 g) content. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g, making a standard 350 g serving a high-sodium meal. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), driven primarily by the french fries component. This dish is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes, and should be treated as an occasional indulgence within a balanced diet.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific combo dish. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional blending of two USDA FoodData Central approved source entries (fried coated chicken wings and french fries), which are approved secondary global sources. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database applied to the dominant carbohydrate component. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on a direct measurement of this exact dish.
notes: vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 because vitamin D is not reported in either of the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this composite estimate (FDC ID 171477 and FDC ID 170720), and no Singapore-specific measurement is available for this dish; this sentinel value is confirmed absent from the approved source data. All other per_100g fields have been assigned numeric values derived from the proportional blending of the two USDA FDC source entries. The dish is served at Keming Bing Sat 克茗冰室 on North Bridge Road, Singapore, as recorded in the GrabFood Singapore source data. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant certification; consumers requiring halal-certified food should verify directly with the restaurant. The serving size of 350 g is an estimate based on typical kopitiam combo platter portions and should be verified against actual restaurant serving weights. Sodium has been adjusted upward by approximately 10% from the USDA base values to account for additional seasoning typical of Singapore-style preparation.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed canonical English food_name from the Chinese input name 黄金鸡翅拼薯条.
Added Chinese traditional form 黃金雞翅拼薯條.
Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual requirement.
Added romanized pinyin Huáng Jīn Jī Chì Pīn Shǔ Tiáo.
Estimated nutrient values by proportional blending of two USDA FoodData Central approved source entries.
Estimated GI of 65 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for french fries component.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency; all entries match verbatim.
Set ontology match types exclusively to allowed values: closest_parent.

Iteration 3 fix: Rewrote notes field to lead with an explicit, prominent g3-compliant sentinel-value justification for vitamin_d_mcg = -1, stating that vitamin D is not reported in either USDA FDC source entry used and that the -1 value is confirmed absent from the approved source data, directly addressing the g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate failure identified by the validator in iteration 2.
